meta.notes: Novel AI-generated PSBB practice questions for the SpeedyCAT proof-of-concept. NOT real MCAT/AAMC content. Text-only. Chunk 5 of 10.

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes material culture?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The physical objects and artifacts that members of a society create and use.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The process by which societies move toward industrial, urban, and rationalized forms.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The mechanisms a society uses to encourage or compel conformity to its norms.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The view that deviance arises when people cannot reach valued goals through legitimate means.

questions.explanation: Material culture is best described as the physical objects and artifacts that members of a society create and use. The other options describe different concepts.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of cultural diffusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A dish from one country becoming a popular menu item in distant nations.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A concert crowd suddenly surging together toward the stage.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A fine issued for littering alongside public praise given for a generous act.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A movement that rejects mainstream consumerism and lives communally off the grid.

questions.explanation: An example of cultural diffusion is: a dish from one country becoming a popular menu item in distant nations. In general, cultural diffusion refers to the spread of cultural traits, ideas, or objects from one group or society to another. The other options illustrate different concepts.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which term is best described as follows: an approach that encourages distinct cultural groups to keep their identities within one society?

choices.label: A
choices.text: acculturation

choices.label: B
choices.text: social control

choices.label: C
choices.text: a counterculture

choices.label: D
choices.text: multiculturalism

questions.explanation: This description matches multiculturalism, which is an approach that encourages distinct cultural groups to keep their identities within one society. The other options name different concepts that do not fit this description.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes norms?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A condition of normlessness in which social norms are weak, unclear, or conflicting.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The rules and expectations that guide the behavior of members of a society.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cultural change that results when two cultures come into continuous firsthand contact.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The tendency to evaluate other cultures by the standards of one's own culture, seen as superior.

questions.explanation: Norms is best described as the rules and expectations that guide the behavior of members of a society. The other options describe different concepts.

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of strain theory?

choices.text: Intense public outrage directed at someone who defrauds a vulnerable elderly person.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A person turning to theft after finding no lawful path to the wealth society prizes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A community losing its shared rules and sense of order after sudden upheaval.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A movement that rejects mainstream consumerism and lives communally off the grid.

questions.explanation: An example of strain theory is: a person turning to theft after finding no lawful path to the wealth society prizes. In general, strain theory refers to the view that deviance arises when people cannot reach valued goals through legitimate means. The other options illustrate different concepts.

questions.stem: In a nation, the wealthiest 10 percent of households own 70 percent of all wealth, while the bottom 50 percent own just 5 percent. Which statement best describes this distribution?

questions.stem: In a study of 250 adults, researchers find a correlation of r = -0.62 between hours of sleep per night and self-reported stress. Which interpretation is best supported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: People who sleep more tend to report more stress.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sleeping more causes stress to drop; the study proves this causal link.

choices.label: C
choices.text: People who sleep more tend to report less stress, a moderately strong negative relationship.

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is essentially no relationship between sleep and stress.

questions.explanation: An r of -0.62 is a moderately strong negative correlation: as sleep rises, stress tends to fall. The sign is negative (so more sleep means less stress), and correlation alone cannot establish causation.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Class A test scores are 70, 72, 71, 69, and 73. Class B scores are 50, 90, 60, 95, and 55. Both classes have a mean near 71. Which statement is best supported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both classes have the same standard deviation because their means are equal.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Standard deviation cannot be compared when two means are equal.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Class A has the larger standard deviation because its scores are more spread out.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Class B has the larger standard deviation because its scores are more spread out around the mean.

questions.explanation: Class A scores cluster tightly near 71, while Class B scores range widely from 50 to 95, so Class B has the larger standard deviation even though the means match.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content
